name: The Chemical Brothers
description:
'''The Chemical Brothers''' are an English [[electronic music]] duo formed by Ed Simons and Tom Rowlands in [[Manchester]] in 1992. They were pioneers in bringing the [[big beat]] genre to the forefront of [[pop culture]].

Originally known as The Dust Brothers, they changed their name due to the existence of [[Dust Brothers|another band with the same name]]. Their first album ''[[Exit Planet Dust]]'' sold over one million copies and debuted at No. 9 on the [[UK Albums Chart]]. After signing to [[Virgin Records]], the duo achieved critical acclaim with their second and third albums ''[[Dig Your Own Hole]]'' (1997) and ''[[Surrender (The Chemical Brothers album)|Surrender]]'' (1999), which both topped the [[UK Albums Chart|UK albums chart]]. Their subsequent albums, ''[[Come with Us]]'' (2003), ''[[Push the Button (The Chemical Brothers album)|Push the Button]]'' (2005) and ''[[We Are the Night (album)|We Are The Night]]'' (2007) all reached number one and further expanded their sound with [[neo-psychedelia]], [[House music|house]], and [[Pop music|pop]] influences.

Across their career, they have collaborated with numerous guest vocalists, including [[Noel Gallagher]], [[Beth Orton]], [[Bernard Sumner]], [[Q-Tip (musician)|Q-Tip]], [[St. Vincent (musician)|St. Vincent]], [[Beck]], and [[Aurora (singer)|Aurora]], the latter of which has further collaborated with Rowlands to form [[Supergroup (music)|supergroup]] Tomora.

With 13 top-20 singles in the UK, including "[[Setting Sun (The Chemical Brothers song)|Setting Sun]]" and "[[Block Rockin' Beats]]" which both reached number one, they have won six [[Grammy Awards]], including [[Best Rock Instrumental Performance]], [[Best Dance Recording]], and [[Best Dance/Electronic Album]] for ''[[No Geography]]'' (2018).
